critical_findings.title: Hardcoded Test Credentials in Demo Script
critical_findings.description: Plain-text credentials (alice@test.com:password123, bob@test.com:password123) exposed in source control
critical_findings.recommendation: Use environment variables; move to .gitignore; store in credential manager

critical_findings.title: Hardcoded Test Credentials in Database Seed File
critical_findings.description: Test database seed contains hardcoded credentials and insecure secretKey
critical_findings.impact: Weak cryptographic keys; credentials leaked in logs
critical_findings.recommendation: Use crypto.randomBytes for keys; remove credential console.log

critical_findings.title: Unvalidated WebSocket Message Execution
critical_findings.description: WebSocket messages trigger page reload without authentication or rate-limiting
critical_findings.impact: Denial of service via malicious WebSocket messages
critical_findings.recommendation: Add HMAC signature validation; implement replay attack prevention

critical_findings.title: Hardcoded Test Password in E2E Helper
critical_findings.description: All E2E tests use same hardcoded password 'pwd12345'
critical_findings.impact: Predictable test credentials; risk if tests run against staging/prod
critical_findings.recommendation: Generate unique passwords using crypto.randomBytes() per test run

high_findings.title: JWT Secret Validation is Weak
high_findings.description: JWT_SECRET checked for minimum 32 characters only; no entropy validation
high_findings.impact: Weak signing keys vulnerable to brute force
high_findings.recommendation: Increase minimum to 64 characters; validate entropy (≥128 bits)

high_findings.title: Soft-Deleted Users Can Still Authenticate
high_findings.description: Although code correctly checks deletedAt, this was previously a vulnerability and needs test coverage
high_findings.impact: Deleted users could authenticate with old tokens
high_findings.recommendation: Add unit tests to verify soft-delete filtering in auth middleware

high_findings.title: Plaintext Token Storage in localStorage
high_findings.description: JWT access tokens stored in plaintext localStorage vulnerable to XSS
high_findings.impact: Token theft via XSS; session hijacking
high_findings.recommendation: Move to httpOnly Secure cookies; use sessionStorage for access token at minimum

high_findings.title: Refresh Token Stored in localStorage Without Encryption
high_findings.description: Long-lived refresh token stored plaintext in localStorage
high_findings.impact: Persistent session hijacking via stolen refresh token
high_findings.recommendation: Move to httpOnly Secure cookie; encrypt if localStorage is necessary

high_findings.title: Missing CSRF Protection Documentation
high_findings.description: CSRF protection strategy not documented; if cookies added later, CSRF tokens required
high_findings.impact: Vulnerability if cookie-based auth is introduced without CSRF tokens
high_findings.recommendation: Document JWT-based CSRF immunity; add explicit CSRF token requirement for future cookie-based auth

high_findings.title: Insufficient Rate-Limiting on Authentication Endpoints
high_findings.description: No strict rate-limiting visible on /auth/login and /auth/signup endpoints
high_findings.recommendation: Add 5 attempts/15min for login; 3 attempts/hour for signup; use Redis for distributed rate-limiting

high_findings.title: No Input Length Validation on Sensitive Fields
high_findings.description: Email, name, and password fields lack maximum length constraints
high_findings.impact: DoS via large payloads; database bloat
high_findings.recommendation: Add max: 254 for email (RFC 5321); max: 128 for password; max: 100 for name

high_findings.title: Missing Server-Side Validation for Enum Fields
high_findings.description: Enum fields (category, status, type) not validated with Zod enum() schema
high_findings.impact: Invalid state in database; application crashes

high_findings.title: No Content-Type Validation in File Uploads
high_findings.description: Proof image uploads don't validate MIME type or file size limits
high_findings.impact: Malware distribution; DoS via large files
high_findings.recommendation: Validate Content-Type header; check magic bytes with file-type library; enforce 5MB max

high_findings.title: Google OAuth Token Not Properly Encrypted
high_findings.description: Google OAuth refresh token stored in database without encryption
high_findings.impact: Unauthorized Google Calendar access if database is compromised
high_findings.recommendation: Encrypt refresh tokens using AES-256-GCM before storage; decrypt on retrieval

high_findings.title: Error Messages Leak System Information
high_findings.description: Full error messages and stack traces exposed in development mode
high_findings.impact: Information disclosure; attacker learns system architecture
high_findings.recommendation: Log errors server-side only; return generic messages to client unless EXPOSE_ERRORS=true

high_findings.title: No Validation of Couple Relationship in Route Handlers
high_findings.description: Protected routes verify authMiddleware but not coupleId ownership (IDOR)
high_findings.impact: User A could access/modify User B's couple data if they guess IDs
high_findings.recommendation: Add coupleId check in all couple-scoped resource queries: where: { id, coupleId: req.coupleId }

medium_findings.title: Unencrypted Sensitive Data in Helper Storage
medium_findings.recommendation: Encrypt with AES-256-GCM before storing to disk

medium_findings.title: Missing HSTS Header
medium_findings.recommendation: Add helmet.hsts({ maxAge: 31536000, includeSubDomains: true, preload: true })

medium_findings.title: Insufficient Password Entropy Requirements
medium_findings.recommendation: Require 12+ chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character

medium_findings.title: No Account Lockout After Failed Login Attempts
medium_findings.recommendation: Lock account for 15 minutes after 5 failed attempts

medium_findings.title: No Security Headers for Content Injection Protection
No Rate-Limiting on Expensive Operations
medium_findings.recommendation: Add per-user rate-limiting on export/expensive operations

low_findings.title: No Version Pinning in Package Manager

immediate_1_week: C-001: Remove hardcoded credentials from DEMO_SCRIPT.js
C-002: Remove plaintext passwords from seed.js
C-004: Generate unique passwords in E2E helpers
short_term_1_month: H-001: Increase JWT_SECRET to 64+ characters with entropy validation
H-003, H-004: Move tokens to httpOnly Secure cookies
H-007, H-008: Add input validation and length limits
H-009: Validate file uploads (MIME type, size)
H-010: Encrypt Google OAuth tokens at rest
H-006: Add rate-limiting to auth endpoints
long_term_3_months: H-012: Audit all routes for IDOR vulnerabilities
M-005, M-006: Implement password complexity + account lockout

dependency_status.note: Run 'npm audit' and 'npx snyk test' to identify vulnerable packages
